Floor exercises replaced with standing equivalents. Same muscle targets, all upright.
Core + Glutes · 10 min · Tap to start ▶
No-Equipment Core & Glutes Circuit
🕐 10 minutes 🔁 2 rounds ⏸ 60 sec rest ~200 cal
Exercises
1
Curtsy Lunge Side Kick
20 sec each side
Step back into a curtsy lunge, then as you rise kick your leg out to the side. Targets outer glutes and hip abductors simultaneously.
2
Bridge and Twist
45 sec
Sit with knees bent, arms behind you. Lift hips off the mat, extend one arm toward the ceiling and twist your torso. Hits glutes, obliques and core.
3
Plank Leg Extension Pulses
20 sec each side
From a plank, extend one leg back and pulse it up. Directly fires the glute from a fully extended position — one of the hardest angles to hit.
4
Squat Thrust
45 sec
Squat down, jump feet back to plank, jump back in, stand up. Full body cardio burst — elevates heart rate and burns extra calories through the circuit.
5
Glute Bridge Overhead Reach
45 sec
Bridge position, hips up, reach one arm up and across the body to touch the floor on the opposite side. Glutes plus rotational core work.
6
Downward Dog Crunch
20 sec each side
From downward dog, bring your knee to your elbow, then extend the leg straight up and back. Core, hip flexors, glutes and shoulder stability.
Core + Abs · 10 min · Tap to start ▶
10-Minute Tummy Tuck Workout
🕐 10 minutes 🔁 2 rounds ⏸ 60 sec rest ~180 cal
Exercises
1
Plank Hip Dips
45 sec
Low plank, rotate hips side to side dipping nearly to the floor. Hammers obliques and deep core without leaving the plank position.
2
Bicycle Crunches
45 sec
On your back, lift shoulders and legs. Bring knee to opposite elbow while extending the other leg. The rotation is what targets the obliques — go slow and deliberate.
3
Dead Bug
45 sec
Arms and legs up, lower opposite arm and leg slowly without arching your back. Trains the deep core stabilizers that actually hold your stomach flat.
4
Straight Leg Raise
45 sec
Hands by sides, legs straight. Raise both legs to 90°, pause, lower slowly. Lower abs and hip flexors — finishes what the dead bug started.
5
Seated Knee Tucks
45 sec
Sit with legs extended, lean back, bring knees to chest and extend again without touching the floor. Constant tension on the lower abs the whole set.
6
Wood Chop
20 sec each side
With or without weight, squat and rotate diagonally from low to high. Rotational core power — hits the obliques through their full range of motion.
Posture + Back · Beginner · Tap to start ▶
Beginner Better Posture Workout
🕐 ~15 minutes 🔁 2 rounds ⏸ 60 sec rest ~150 cal 🏋️ Dumbbells
💡 Good posture visually pulls in your waist, lengthens your torso, and makes your whole silhouette look leaner — without any actual fat loss. This routine fixes the rounded shoulders that hiding your waist shape.
Exercises
1
Arm Circles
30 sec each direction
Arms out to sides, make small forward circles then reverse. Warms up the shoulder joint and activates the rear deltoids that pull your shoulders back.
2
Dumbbell Bent Over Row
45 sec
Hinge forward, pull dumbbells to waist squeezing shoulder blades. The #1 exercise for strengthening the upper back muscles that hold your posture upright.
3
Chest Fly
60 sec
On your back, arms up, lower out to sides until you feel a chest stretch. Balances the row by opening a tight chest that pulls shoulders forward.
4
Bird Dogs
60 sec
On hands and knees, extend opposite arm and leg, hold 3–5 sec. Trains the spinal stabilizers that keep your back neutral when standing all day.
5
Dumbbell Shoulder Press
45 sec
Dumbbells at shoulders, palms forward, press overhead fully. Strengthens the shoulder stabilizers that keep your posture upright under load.
6
Dumbbell Pullover
60 sec
On your back, one dumbbell overhead and lower behind head. Stretches the lats and chest simultaneously — one of the best thoracic spine openers.
7
Bent Over Lateral Raise
45 sec
Hinge forward, raise arms out to sides to shoulder height. Targets the rear deltoids directly — the muscles most responsible for pulling shoulders back.
8
T Press
45 sec
Lie on belly, arms in T shape. Lift torso and arms, squeeze shoulder blades, hold. Directly strengthens the mid-back muscles that support upright posture.
Yoga · Full Body · Recovery
Full Body Yoga Flow
🕐 17 minutes 🔁 2 rounds 🧘 Breathe deeply ~120 cal
💡 Great for after-work or rest days. Yoga decompresses your spine after standing all day, improves hip flexibility which directly makes your glute exercises more effective, and lowers cortisol which reduces belly fat storage.
Poses — scroll the image above for the full visual guide
1
Mountain Pose
60 sec
Stand tall, feet together, palms together at heart. Sets your baseline posture and brings awareness to how you\'re standing before you start moving.
2
Chair Pose
45 sec
Arms up, sit back into a squat. Strengthens thighs and glutes while opening the chest — yoga's version of a squat hold.
3
Standing Forward Bend
60 sec
Bend forward from the hips, let the spine decompress. Stretches the hamstrings and lower back — essential after a shift on your feet.
4
Downward Facing Dog
45 sec
Hips up and back, heels pressing down, arms long. Full posterior chain stretch — calves, hamstrings, spine and shoulders all at once.
5
Dolphin Pose
30 sec
Forearms on mat, hips up. Deeper shoulder stretch than downward dog — good for undoing the tension from standing with arms at sides all day.
6
Plank + Side Plank
45 sec + 30 sec each side
Hold plank, then roll to side plank. Core stabilization and oblique hold — active strength work within the yoga flow.
7
Child's Pose
45 sec
Sit back on heels, arms forward, forehead down. Lower back decompression and hip flexor stretch — your spine will thank you.
8
High Lunge + Warrior I + Warrior II
30 sec each, both sides
Deep hip flexor and glute stretch through lunge progressions. These poses open the hips in ways that directly improve glute activation in your other workouts.
9
Bridge Pose
30 sec
Feet flat, hips up, chest open. Gentle glute activation and spinal extension — the yoga version of a glute bridge hold.
10
Supine Spinal Twist + Wind Release + Corpse Pose
30 sec each, close with 60 sec rest
Floor finishers — spinal rotation, knee hugs, full relaxation. These are where the actual recovery happens. Don't skip them.
